title: Move '+ Add Item' to the top of a Group
excerpt:
The option to move ‘+ Add Item’ to the top of a Group.  Currently, it’s at the bottom and you need to scroll all the way down to add/see new Items. 
Ex. Someone assigns you a task and, using automations, an Item is creat…

posts.author: Gabriela.Monday (Gabriela)
posts.cookedHtml:
The option to move ‘+ Add Item’ to the top of a Group. Currently, it’s at the bottom and you need to scroll all the way down to add/see new Items.

Ex. Someone assigns you a task and, using automations, an Item is created on your Board. If the new Item/task is at the bottom, you could miss seeing it.

Thank you, Monday, for the chance to submit requests.

posts.author: gbarker121 (Geoffrey Barker)
posts.cookedHtml: When a group has a lot of items, there is a lot of unnecessary scrolling to get to the Add Item box. It would improve efficiency if there was a way to move the Add Item box to the top of a group (or if the top was the default location).

posts.author: tswartz (Theo)
posts.cookedHtml: Seconded. It feels inefficient to have this at the bottom.

posts.author: editor_in_chief (Adam Ruggiero)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking for the ability to add a new pulse at the top of a group, rather than bottom (current default). Currently, expanding a collapsed group to add a new pulse requires user to scroll all the way to the bottom of said group — for big groups, this is inefficient.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, thank you for your feature request! We wanted to let you know that we will be merging this topic existing feature request - Move '+ Add Item' to the top of a Group. - in order to consolidate matching threads and ensure votes are accurate and up-to-date. If you did not cast a vote on this topic, please add your vote to the active thread above. Thanks for your understanding

posts.author: Benz (Braden)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a Monday.com user, I want to see the ‘Add Item’ CTA at the top of the group, so that I don’t have to scroll all the way to the bottom, just to add an item.

If my group has a large number of items, I have to scroll all the way to the bottom just to add a new item. I should be able to add a new item without having to scroll.

posts.author: mzona (Marisa Zona)
posts.cookedHtml:
Having the option to set new items to appear at the top or bottom on the board (groups) will improve user experience. We prefer all new item to default to the top of the list.

We use sort and save however it never seems to stick.

posts.author: CuriousFrog (JD Gargano)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is it possible to have the devs add something simple to the platform that has the ADD NEW ITEM/TASK/LEAD/etc. to the very top of each group? That way we don’t have to scroll all the way down to add something new to a group that has dozens and dozens or more of items in that group? As an example, one of my clients has so many leads in a group that to add a new one it’s a daunting task to have to keep scrolling all the way down every time they need to add it.

Also, could we add a shortcut that is ADD NEW ITEM/LEAD/ETC so when we hit that, it goes right to that Add New cell?

posts.author: amyREagent (Amy)
posts.cookedHtml: any update on this being put into place?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone!

I have checked in with the product team and shared your concerns. At this stage, I am not aware of any changes being made to this feature, however, if any information is shared, I will be sure to update the thread

posts.author: Michael-Burns (Michae Burns)
posts.cookedHtml:
For those who are still waiting for Monday’s team to implement this feature I have a work around.

Unfortunately we can’t edit the “Main table” which is another issue, but follow these steps to implement.

  1. You need to create a column in your items that is synced to creation date. ( lock this column so nobody can touch it )
  2. You can then create a filter/sort view with the date descending.
  3. Once it works and your items are inverted (now showing Newest at top) save view as “new board” In my case I called it “Primary Table”
  4. Then simply set this new view (primary table) as default and delete your old “Main Table”
  5. your new default view when entering this board should have a saved sort feature inverting your items by creation date.

Feel free to call your saved view what ever you like, once there was no duplicate naming I changed “primary table” to “main table” so that it matched the existing user behaviour for my team.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: greentoothbrush (ian ross)
posts.cookedHtml: How do you delete the old “Main Table”?

posts.author: Michael-Burns (Michae Burns)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately, you cannot do that.
So what I normally do is use that as my unfiltered board.

It is irritating, but such is the UX of Monday in a lot of cases. I imagine there is a strange reason we cannot delete it.

posts.author: leonidas (Leonidas)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can click on the three-dot menu to the left of an item and select “Create new item below”. This is why they haven’t added your proposal as a feature because it would take up space at the top of the board which would result in an empty line at the top of the board for no reason.

Here are instructions:
https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005319105-The-basics-of-items#:~:text=You%20can%20add%20a%20new%20item%20to%20the%20bottom%20of,press%20Enter%20on%20your%20keyboard.

posts.author: ivofaase (Ivo Faase)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @monday-team Any news if this feature will be added? By default I would like to add new items on top. Currently I always have to scroll down, add a task and then drag it up manually. It would be great if users just have the option to choose if they want it at the top or at the bottom.

posts.author: Lucas1 (Lucas)
posts.cookedHtml:
This only works with the top group, not other groups. And I personally hadnt realised this option was there so I think they could make it more obvious.

Also if the empty line would be a problem for them (I agree it would be) why do I have this waste of space at the top currently?;

image1807×111 16.2 KB

posts.author: micheese (Mich)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to add tasks at the top of my group so I would like request to add in the “…” menu of a task/item to “Create a new task above” or to somehow more easily add a new task at the top of my group list.

posts.author: SevenDayPermits (Rob)
posts.cookedHtml:
We consistently have 100+ items on a board, each item is a project. When adding a new project to the board (a new item) I need to scroll down to the bottom as that is where it defaults. Can we have the option to add to the top of the board, so when I add an item I can simply start filling it out?

Image 1: add item
Image 2: adds to the bottom
Image 3: awesome if it would add to the top so we do not need to scroll through the 100+ other items to get to it.

1443×496 42.6 KB

2437×313 21.8 KB

3392×496 35.9 KB

Not sure if I am missing something or if this is just how it works. Thought this was a pain for a while now, figured I would put it out there and see if

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey! Shannon here with the monday team. Thank you for submitting your feature request in April! To ensure that votes are accurately counted for feature requests, I merged your request with this one here.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Rob! Thank you so much for submitting your feature request, and for providing the visuals for additional context! I’ve merged your request with this existing one here to ensure that votes are accurately counted. This way, we can centralize votes for this particular request.
